meaning: n.踢马剌,剌激物,(鸟,虫等的)距,刺激v.鞭策,刺激,疾驰,驱策
examples: This has forced airlines to discount fares heavily in order to spur demand.
Their attitude, rather than reining him back, only seemed to spur Philip on.
Let the cheers from the crowd spur you on.
It also helped spur the Federal Election Commission to bar the use of campaign contributions for non-campaign activities.
Kennedy hoped in the aftermath of the bombing that it would spur action against racial hatred.
